text.title: AI Feature De-Risking — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI-feature de-risking pipeline enforces: kill-criteria pre-registered, frozen eval set, statistical reporting, judge recalibration, and bounded eval cost.

rule.statement: Kill-criteria (pass / fail / freeze threshold) MUST be written and committed BEFORE the first eval run. Adjusting the threshold after seeing the result invalidates the comparison.

rationale.source: Pre-registration practice; Wagenmakers et al. 2012 on garden-of-forking-paths

rule.rationale: Without pre-registered thresholds, teams unconsciously rationalise borderline results into "ship". The threshold ahead of time is the only honest signal.

rule.statement: The ground-truth set MUST be versioned and frozen before each comparison run. Adding examples mid-comparison invalidates the comparison.
rule.rationale: Mutable eval sets produce non-comparable metrics. A score change 0.82 → 0.85 is meaningless if the denominator changed.

rule.statement: Each eval report MUST include n, mean, and confidence interval — never a single number.

rationale.source: Wasserstein & Lazar, ASA Statement on p-values 2016

rule.rationale: Naked mean ignores sampling noise. 0.82 ± 0.08 over n=30 is a different story than 0.82 ± 0.01 over n=1000.

rule.statement: Judge-LLM scoring MUST be recalibrated against ≥20 human-labeled examples per quarter. Judge drift invalidates downstream metrics.

rationale.source: Zheng et al., MT-Bench 2023; LLM-as-judge drift studies

rule.rationale: Judge models change. Without recalibration the eval becomes a measurement of the judge, not the target.

rule.statement: Per-example eval cost MUST be logged. Any eval whose run-cost exceeds 2× the production daily traffic is downsampled OR moved to nightly OR demoted to staging.

rationale.source: FinOps for ML; FinOps Foundation guidance

rule.rationale: Unbounded eval budgets break the cost case and reduce eval frequency, defeating the purpose.

example.title: Versioned eval report
example.code: kill_criteria: pass-rate >= 0.80 (set 2026-04-10) set: golden-v3-frozen-2026-05-15 (n=247) model: claude-opus-4.6 prompt: jtbd-synthesis@a3f1c (git SHA) result: pass-rate 0.83 ± 0.04 (95% CI) verdict: PASS (above threshold; CI lower bound 0.79 marginal)
example.note: Every element of the comparison is pinned and pre-registered.

example.title: Naked eval result
example.note: Unknown set, unknown model, no CI, no pre-registered threshold. Useless for regression detection.
